# Sparrow Wallet

> Modern desktop Bitcoin wallet supporting most hardware wallets, built on PSBT with emphasis on transparency and usability.

## Repository Structure

- `src/main/java/com/sparrowwallet/sparrow/` — Main application source
  - `control/` — JavaFX UI controllers (133 files): wallet management, transaction display, settings, UTXO management, server configuration
  - `wallet/` — Wallet models and logic (27 files): wallets, UTXOs, labels, payments
  - `net/` — Network layer (50 files): Electrum server connection, Bitcoin Core RPC, Tor integration, payment protocol (BIP70/BIP21/BIP353)
  - `transaction/` — Transaction handling (17 files): PSBT creation, signing, broadcasting, RBF, CPFP
  - `settings/` — Application settings (7 files): network, server, display preferences
  - `io/` — File/import/export (71 files): wallet file persistence, CSV export, PDF generation, image handling
  - `event/` — Event system (144 files): application-wide event bus using EventBus pattern
  - `keystoreimport/` — Hardware wallet keystore imports: Ledger, Trezor, Coldcard, BitBox, Jade, Specter, Cobo, Keystone
  - `payjoin/` — BIP78 Payjoin implementation (2 files)
  - `paynym/` — PayNym (BIP47) implementation (6 files)
  - `glyphfont/` — FontAwesome icon bindings (3 files)
  - `terminal/` — Bitcoin RPC terminal interface
  - `instance/` — Single-instance management (prevents multiple Sparrow processes)
  - `AppServices.java` — Application services bootstrap and lifecycle
  - `SparrowWallet.java` — Main application entry point
  - `AppController.java` — Primary UI controller
- `drongo/` — Submodule: core Bitcoin library (https://github.com/sparrowwallet/drongo)
  - PSBT, descriptors, scripts, transactions, policies, HD keys, address derivation
- `lark/` — Submodule: Bitcoin USB hardware wallet interface library (https://github.com/sparrowwallet/lark)
  - Communication protocols for Ledger, Trezor, Coldcard, BitBox, Jade, Specter, Cobo, Keystone
- `docs/` — Documentation (README.md, reproducible.md for deterministic builds)
- `build.gradle` — Gradle build configuration (Java 25, JavaFX, jlink packaging)
- `settings.gradle` — Multi-project settings (root + drongo + lark)

## Build and Development

Requires Java 25 or higher (release built with Eclipse Temurin 25.0.2+10).

```bash
# Clone with submodules
git clone --recursive https://github.com/sparrowwallet/sparrow.git

# Build release binary
./gradlew jpackage

# Run from source
./sparrow

# Update submodules after pull
git pull --recurse-submodules
```

Additional packages may be needed: `rpm fakeroot binutils` (Debian/Ubuntu).

Windows installer requires [WiX](https://github.com/wixtoolset/wix3/releases).

## Supported Networks

mainnet, testnet (testnet3), testnet4, regtest, signet.

Set via CLI flag `--network` or environment variable `SPARROW_NETWORK`.

## Key Dependencies

- JavaFX (controls, fxml, swing, graphics)
- drongo (Bitcoin primitives library, submodule)
- lark (hardware wallet USB interface, submodule)
- Guava, Gson, H2 database, JDBI, Flyway (migrations)
- ZXing (QR codes), JCommander (CLI), RichTextFX (text rendering)
- Tor (via `io.matthewnelson.kmp.tor` resource filter)

## Code Style

- Conventional lowercase imperative commit messages (e.g., "fix regression to restore save pdf button")
- Java package structure: `com.sparrowwallet.sparrow.<domain>`
- JavaFX controller-per-view pattern using FXML
- Event-driven architecture with EventBus for cross-controller communication
- No checkstyle, PMD, or spotbugs configured
- No CLA, DCO, or GPG signing required for contributions

## Testing

```bash
# Run tests
./gradlew test
```

Test directory: `src/test/java/com/sparrowwallet/sparrow/` (primarily `io/` tests).

## CI/CD

- `.github/workflows/package.yaml` — Build and package for Windows, Linux (x64/arm), macOS (Intel/ARM)
  - Triggered by `workflow_dispatch` only (no push-triggered CI)
  - Uses JDK 25.0.2 (Eclipse Temurin)
  - macOS builds include code signing and notarization

## Reproducible Builds

Binary reproducibility supported from v1.5.0 onwards (pre-codesigning and installer packaging).
See `docs/reproducible.md` for instructions.
